assertions.text: V1 was traveling east in the first of four lanes on the roadway (implies V1 was moving east prior to impact).
V2 was traveling east in the same lane ahead of V1 (implies V2 was moving east prior to impact).
The front of V1 contacted the back of V2 (rear-end impact by V1 into V2).
V2 was redirected off the right side of the road (V2 moved rightward off the roadway after impact).
The right side of V2 contacted the concrete traffic barrier (V2's right side struck the barrier).
Both vehicles then came to rest in the road (both V1 and V2 ended at final rest in the roadway).
V2 at final rest had a major fire (a major fire occurred at V2's final rest).
There was at least one impact: V1's front contacting V2's rear (single distinct initial impact).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows V1 speed was [first_value 253.0 km/h](T2) at t=[-5.0 s](T1) and [last_value 203.0 km/h](T2) at t=[-0.5 s](T1), indicating V1 was moving prior to impact.
Telemetry is only from V1; there is no direct sensor data for V2's motion in the V1 dataset, so V2's pre-impact travel direction cannot be determined from these sensors.

The EDR crash pulse shows a large negative longitudinal delta-V peaking at [value_at_max -89.0 km/h](T2) at t=[0.14 s](T2), consistent with a rear-end impact where V1 experienced a strong forward-to-rearward change (V1's front hitting another vehicle).

The lateral cumulative delta-V for V1 is [final_value -22.0 km/h](T2) (peak [value_at_max -23.0 km/h](T2)), indicating V1 experienced a leftward/rightward device-frame lateral impulse, but vehicle-frame direction is unverifiable; additionally, there is no V2 lateral track data so V2 being redirected off the right side cannot be confirmed by V1 telemetry.
Contact details for V2 (which side hit the barrier) are not recorded in V1 telemetry; sensors do not record V2's contact with a concrete barrier.

Final rest positions of V2 are not available in V1 telemetry; V1 speed drops to [final_value -86.0 km/h cumulative delta-V](T2) during crash pulse but that does not state final rest locations for both vehicles.
Fire occurrence at V2 is not recorded in V1 telemetry; no sensor channels indicate a post-crash fire.

The EDR summary records [num_events_recorded 1](T1) event and the crash pulse shows a single pronounced longitudinal delta-V peak [value_at_max -89.0 km/h](T2) at t=[0.14 s](T2), consistent with a single distinct initial impact.

result.rationale: V1 telemetry supports that V1 was moving prior to impact and that V1 experienced a single, large longitudinal crash pulse consistent with a rear-end collision; however, assertions about V2's motion, contacts, final positions, and fire are not recorded by V1 sensors and are therefore unverifiable.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
